assertions.expectation: Recommends Stable or Extended release channel to minimize upgrade frequency
assertions.reason: Explicitly recommends Extended channel with detailed explanation of why it provides maximum control over minor versions and minimizes upgrade frequency

assertions.expectation: Recommends 'no minor or node upgrades' maintenance exclusion to block disruptive upgrades while allowing security patches on the control plane
assertions.reason: Shows specific gcloud commands to add maintenance exclusion with scope 'no_minor_or_node_upgrades' and explains this allows CP security patches while blocking disruptive upgrades

assertions.expectation: Recommends a narrow maintenance window (e.g., weekends only) so CP patches happen at predictable times
assertions.reason: Configures Saturday early morning 4-hour maintenance window with specific gcloud commands for predictable timing

assertions.expectation: Suggests isolating training workloads on dedicated GPU node pools separate from serving/infrastructure pools
assertions.reason: Creates separate h100-training and h100-inference node pools with different configurations and upgrade strategies

assertions.expectation: Recommends disabling auto-upgrade on GPU training node pools and upgrading manually during scheduled gaps between training runs
assertions.reason: Shows workflow for manual upgrades between training campaigns and uses maintenance exclusions to prevent automatic upgrades during training periods

assertions.expectation: Advises configuring training frameworks with checkpointing so jobs can resume after any disruption
assertions.reason: Includes training job YAML with checkpoint storage volume mounts and 1-hour termination grace period for checkpoint saving

assertions.expectation: Mentions GPU driver version testing — always validate in staging before applying to production GPU pools
assertions.reason: Does not mention GPU driver version testing or validation in staging environment before production upgrades

assertions.expectation: Notes that GPUDirect/RDMA networking has strict GKE version requirements — verify compatibility before upgrading
assertions.reason: Mentions RDMA topology with compact placement but does not discuss version compatibility requirements for GPUDirect/RDMA networking
